# Scope + allowlist for the host-purity DELTA guard.
# Prefixed globs, one per line; '#' comments and blank lines ignored.
#
# Precedence is a TOTAL, per-path rule (not first-match): allow > exclude > include.
# A path is scanned iff it matches some `include` glob, matches no `exclude` glob,
# AND matches no `allow` glob. `allow` therefore overrides both `include` and
# `exclude`, which is what lets `allow scripts/webhook/**` carve a pending-MOVE
# exemption out of `include scripts/**` regardless of line order.
#
# Glob semantics (scanner-applied, not git-pathspec): `**` matches any characters
# including `/`; a single `*` matches within one path segment (not `/`); every
# other character is literal.

# host tool / mechanism files that must stay service-agnostic
include .claude/hooks/**
include .claude/agents/**
include .claude/workflows/**
include scripts/**
include setup/**
include .github/workflows/**
include Jenkinsfile
include *.sh

# out of scope (exclude tier = structurally not host-tool surface): prose
# legitimately names services; tests carry token fixtures. docs/** (incl. ADR /
# decoupling-plan) is EXCLUDE, not allow — the "docs pass" property is an
# exclude-tier property, not an allow-tier exemption; the two tiers are kept
# distinct for legibility.
exclude docs/**
exclude tests/**
exclude scripts/test/**
exclude .autoflow/**

# live-product tooling pending MOVE (host-service-decoupling-plan.md §10). NOTE:
# the tokens fixture tests/fixtures/host-purity-tokens.txt is NOT listed here — it
# is already covered by `exclude tests/**` and matches no `include` glob, so an
# `allow` for it would be a dead entry. `allow` is reserved for paths that DO match
# an include glob and must be carved back out (the pending-MOVE set under
# `include scripts/**`).
allow scripts/webhook/**
allow scripts/seed/**
allow scripts/poc-smoke.sh
allow scripts/live-smoke.sh
allow scripts/check-env-contract.sh
allow infra/**
allow clients/**

# generated artifact (issue #792): setup/manifest.json is emitted by
# setup/gen-manifest-hashes.sh and its rows verbatim-mirror docs/** paths (the
# markdown-link doc closure, e.g. docs/adr/0001-...librechat-submodule-boundary.md).
# docs/** is exclude-tier because prose/paths legitimately name services; the
# generated mirror of those paths inherits that property. Hand-authored setup
# logic (init.sh, thin-root-layer/*, gen-manifest-hashes.sh) stays in scope
# under `include setup/**`.
allow setup/manifest.json
